title: My Work not able to see all boards?
excerpt: I am trying to use mywork as a todo list.  But it’s only listing tasks from 3 boards.  In settings, it does not see any other boards.  Do I need to do something to my other boards to make them visible there?

posts.author: jMonday (jed davidow)
posts.cookedHtml: I am trying to use mywork as a todo list. But it’s only listing tasks from 3 boards. In settings, it does not see any other boards. Do I need to do something to my other boards to make them visible there?

posts.author: CaseOfTheMondays (Tanya Blankenship)
posts.cookedHtml: You may have to search for your boards by name. From my experience, they do not always show up in a selectable list.

posts.author: tommemben (Tom Charde)
posts.cookedHtml: We’ve been having this same issue for more than a year. I’ve exchanged dozens of emails with Monday Support, but there has been no resolution.

posts.author: tommemben (Tom Charde)
posts.cookedHtml: You can’t search for boards within My Work.

posts.author: maxtownsend (Max Townsend)
posts.cookedHtml: Following. Same issue - all boards not showing in My Work.

posts.author: lissi77 (Alix Horsch)
posts.cookedHtml: I have the same issue. Has there been some solution or resolution of this by now?

posts.author: tommemben (Tom Charde)
posts.cookedHtml:
Not that I know of. We ended up creating new boards and transferring pulses. We also had the same issue with certain pulses not showing up, and had to recreate those as well.

Apparently Monday changed something in the code a few years ago. One support rep suggested that these issues seem to be isolated to boards/pulses that were created using the old code. They worked on the old “My Week” page, but some aren’t compatible with the newer version of that (“My Work”.) And because this has only affected a small number of users (early adopters), they haven’t really prioritized addressing it.

posts.author: Stevenmeyers (Steve Meyers)
posts.cookedHtml: I also cannot see all my boards in My Work. This makes the My Work feature useless. I’ve tried copying boards, renaming boards, etc. There is no way to search for a board in My Work. This is a Bug that should be addressed immediately.

posts.author: thestaceyharris (Stacey Harris)
posts.cookedHtml: Still seems to be an issue, and it seems to vary between users. I can see boards other members of my team cannot, AND there are a couple of newer boards no one seems to be able to see. Is Monday.com just going to ignore this massive issue?

posts.author: Nat (Nathalie Sarlin)
posts.cookedHtml: I am also having this issue and it is causing major problems.

posts.author: daniel.king.redcross (Daniel J King)
posts.cookedHtml: Monday.com needs to fix this, as the My Work feature is badly needed but currently broken.

posts.author: daniel.king.redcross (Daniel J King)
posts.cookedHtml: In case this helps anyone else here – tech support from Monday clarified that you have to be assigned to something on every board you want to see in My Work

posts.author: chadhughes (Chad Hughes)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is pretty ridiculous. As a manager I need to see what’s going on but we can’t even see the list of boards? Seriously? What’s the point of this whole system if there’s no way to see the whole??

Why can’t we select ‘see all boards’ and then carve them out? Are we expected to memorize the dozens of boards we have and manually add them each time?

posts.author: peter15682 (Peter Lemaire)
posts.cookedHtml: I am incredibly late to the party here, but one thing I found is that boards only show up from My Work to check off IF I have already been assigned to something on that board. Put a different way, My Work will only allow you to filter from boards that you are assigned to. This has solved the issue most of the time for me.
